explanation: The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory phase ends while the system is in PCV mode, the switch to PSV mode is selected at that very moment. This directly translates the temporal condition 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' to the consequent action 'the switch to PSV shall occur'.

The LTL property states that globally, if the inspiratory phase ends while the system is in PCV mode, then in the next time step, the switch to PSV mode shall be initiated. This captures the meaning of "The switch to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time" by interpreting "at the end of" as a trigger that leads to an immediate state change in the following step (represented by the 'X' operator).

The property states that globally (G), whenever the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the system is currently in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), then the switch to Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSVModeSelected) must be initiated at that same time. This captures the synchronous occurrence implied by 'at the end of'.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory phase ends and the ventilator is in PCV mode, the switch to PSV mode occurs at that same moment. This directly translates 'The switch to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time'.

The property states that globally (G), if the inspiratory phase ends while the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the PSV mode must be selected (bool_exp3). This directly translates the requirement that the switch to PSV occurs at the end of a PCV inspiratory time.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode ('PCVMode') and the inspiratory phase ends ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), then in the very next state (X), the switch to PSV mode ('PSVModeSelected') shall occur. This captures the requirement that the transition to PSV happens precisely at the end of a PCV inspiratory time.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory phase ends AND the system is in PCV mode, the switch to PSV mode shall occur. This captures the requirement that the switch to PSV happens precisely at the end of a PCV inspiratory time.

The property states that globally, whenever the switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), it must be at the exact moment when the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode). This captures the timing constraint 'shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' on the 'switch to PSV'.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory phase ends while the system is in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) Mode, a switch to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode must occur at that exact moment. This directly translates "The switch to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time", implying a concurrent event.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory phase ends (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`) while the ventilator is in PCV mode (`PCVMode`), then in the very next time step (`X`), the switch to PSV mode (`PSVModeSelected`) must occur. This captures the strict temporal requirement for the switch.
